description: Splitting a system into services before the domain boundaries are understood, turning method calls into brittle network coupling.
body: Failure mode: a codebase is decomposed into services or packages along guessed boundaries, so what would have been a refactor becomes cross-network changes, distributed transactions, and chatty calls along seams that turned out wrong. Detection signals: frequent lockstep deploys across services; cross-service transactions; boundaries that change often. Do not commit to hard process boundaries before the domain is stable. Safer fallback: keep boundaries as in-process modules with narrow contracts first, validate them through real change pressure, and extract a service only once the seam proves stable.
